L1.area: Runtime permission prompting and user interaction subsystem

L2.description: A race condition vulnerability exists in the interactive permission prompt mechanism. In a multi-threaded context, concurrent code can write to standard output and error streams while a security-sensitive prompt is being displayed to the user. This allows a malicious program to clear the terminal screen and replace the permission prompt with misleading text, tricking the user into granting permissions they did not intend to grant.

L3.area: Runtime permission prompting and user interaction subsystem
L3.description: A race condition vulnerability exists in the interactive permission prompt mechanism. In a multi-threaded context, concurrent code can write to standard output and error streams while a security-sensitive prompt is being displayed to the user. This allows a malicious program to clear the terminal screen and replace the permission prompt with misleading text, tricking the user into granting permissions they did not intend to grant.

ground_truth.reason: The interactive permission prompt does not lock standard I/O streams, allowing concurrent threads (e.g., Web Workers) to overwrite the prompt with misleading text and trick users into granting unintended permissions.
